title: tolpis succulenta subsp. filiformis
intro: Tolpis succulenta subsp. filiformis, also known as the 'narrow-leaved Tolpis', is a subspecies of succulent plants in the dandelion family (Asteraceae) native to the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various health conditions such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Tolpis succulenta subsp. filiformis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiseptic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare infusions and tinctures, which are often used in herbal medicine to address a range of health concerns.
